_clinical_notes: Key differentials: (1) Mallet finger: cannot extend DIP joint (fingertip droops) = extensor tendon rupture = splinting required = seek care. (2) Jersey finger: cannot flex DIP (can't curl fingertip) = FDP avulsion = surgical if complete = seek care. (3) Volar plate injury: hyperextension PIP, most common, Grade I-II conservative with buddy taping. (4) Visible deformity/dislocation = seek care. (5) Thumb UCL (Skier's/Gamekeeper's): valgus stress to thumb MCP, instability = seek care if Grade III (complete).

deformity_finger.prompt: Look at the injured finger. Is it visibly bent, crooked, or clearly out of position?

options.label: Yes, looks deformed or out of place
Index, middle, ring, or pinky finger
Bent toward the palm or sideways (e.g., fell on thumb, ball hit thumb)
Jammed straight on (e.g., catching a ball)

thumb_instability.prompt: Does the thumb feel unstable — like it gives way when you try to pinch?

options.label: Knuckle at the base of the thumb (MCP joint)
Middle joint (PIP — second knuckle from tip)

pip_mechanism.prompt: How was the PIP joint (middle knuckle) injured?

finger_extend.prompt: Can you fully straighten (extend) the tip of the finger?

options.label: Fingertip droops — cannot straighten the tip

finger_flex.prompt: Can you curl the tip of the injured finger (bend the fingertip down)?

options.label: Yes, can curl it (may be painful)
Cannot curl the fingertip — it won't bend

severity_finger.prompt: How is the swelling and pain?

options.label: Moderate swelling, movement is painful but possible

outcome.seek_care_reason: A visibly deformed or dislocated finger needs to be examined and reduced by a medical professional. Do not attempt to reduce it yourself.
An unstable thumb after a valgus injury may indicate a complete UCL tear (Skier's/Gamekeeper's Thumb). This often requires surgical evaluation.
Inability to straighten the fingertip indicates a mallet finger (extensor tendon rupture). This requires splinting for 6-8 weeks and needs medical evaluation.
Inability to curl the fingertip may indicate a Jersey Finger (FDP tendon avulsion). Complete tears often require surgical repair and need prompt evaluation.

outcome.injury_subtype: Finger Sprain / Volar Plate Injury - Grade I
Finger Sprain / Volar Plate Injury - Grade II
